Brathydium rugelianum is classified within the class Rhynchonellata, which is a group of extinct marine brachiopods. It is further classified under the order Rhynchonellida. intro: Brathydium rugelianum, also known as Chinese foxglove or yellow foxglove, is a perennial plant native to China, belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Brathydium rugelianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, with the most common preparations being a decoction made from the roots and a topical ointment made from the leaves.
